mnemonics: Static Interface Methods: Like adding a universal tool to a toolbox. The toolbox (interface) defines what tools (methods) must exist, and now it also includes a ready-to-use, shared tool (static method) that doesn't require building a new tool from the blueprint (implementing the class).
keyPoints: Static methods in interfaces are defined with the `static` keyword.
They are called using the interface name (e.g., `InterfaceName.staticMethodName();`).
They cannot be overridden by implementing classes.
They are used to provide utility methods related to the interface's purpose.
They help avoid creating separate utility classes.
commonMistakes: Trying to override a static method from an interface in an implementing class (it hides, not overrides).
Attempting to call the static method on an instance of a class that implements the interface (must use the interface name).
Forgetting that static methods in interfaces are not inherited by classes or other interfaces.
